pattern: The minimum insertions needed equals the string length minus the length of its longest palindromic subsequence, which itself equals the LCS of the string with its reverse.
hints: Think about what characters you do NOT need to insert — those already form a palindrome. The more of them, the fewer insertions required.
The longest palindromic subsequence of s is the same as the longest common subsequence of s and its reverse; characters in that LCS never need a mirror insertion.
Compute LCS(s, reverse(s)) with standard O(n^2) DP, then the answer is len(s) minus that LCS length.

edge_inputs.description: already a palindrome — zero insertions needed
single character is trivially a palindrome
two distinct characters — one insertion needed
no shared characters between halves — must mirror the whole half
almost-palindrome with one mismatch in the middle

stress.description: random lowercase string of length up to 200
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(1, size)
    return [''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(n))]
